item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the first image could not be sourced, and the second image comes from here and is All Rights Reserved. Thus, the images have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't understand why this article has so little of upvotes, this is clearly one of the best SCPs. I'd really love to listen to The Volgun telling this one

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know why, but radio towers have always been creepy for me. Needless to say, this rattled my bones in a way I didn't expect. Nicely done.

+1

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer expired: Edits below have been made. This edit can be reverted by the author at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 72 hours if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
- hide block

SCP-084 and SCP-2675 share several similarities in the form of emitting radiation and the ability to instantly manifest in other locations, making observation efforts difficult. Due to these similarities, I think incorporating a link to SCP-2675 in this passage taken from SCP-084 would be appropriate.

Editing this passage:

SCP-084 appears inside this area at random points, appearing to “jump” at random times, sometimes even appearing in multiple locations at once inside the active area. Internally, the space appears to be unlimited, with SCP-084 at the “center”.

To read:

SCP-084 appears inside this area at random points, appearing to “jump” at random times, sometimes even appearing in multiple locations at once inside the active area. Internally, the space appears to be unlimited, with SCP-084 at the “center”.

Since there are already several things tying these two together, I'd like to add a link to the content that is already present rather than making any new additions.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This will cause relative distances to randomly extend or contract in a “flicker”, causing buildings or objects to suddenly “jump” thousands of meters away, or “rush up” to other points, sometimes even causing “overlaps”. These “overlaps” have a markedly detrimental effect on living tissue.

I love this SCP now. I just love reality distorting things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was pretty unsettling; upvoted in spite of the data expunged in the containment procedures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God, this one still gives me chills.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a brilliant article. +1

However my only problem is this:

Any recordings collected [DATA EXPUNGED].

If I remember correctly, aren't important containment procedures not expunged so that the Foundation's personnel is able to contain it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad I can give this its 200th upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, this reminds me of a nightmare I used to have as a kid- a cloudy sky and a grassy field. Once you enter, you can never come out. You're bringing back scary memories :(

But good job, since it looks like that's what you were trying!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty good, liked it. One nitpick: too much "".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: how do you know all this stuff about the town if it has only been seen once?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I know this is an old post I'm replying to, it is worth noting that such a book does exist. Titled, "Roadside Picnic" it is about people surviving within "visitation" zones where alien technology has wreaked havoc upon what humans understand as physics.

This book inspired the game series, "S.T.A.L.K.E.R."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow.

clapclapclapclapclapclap

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shitballs, this is really excellent. I don't recall reading it before today but I seriously missed out if that's the case. The clinchers were the photo of the deer and its accompanying caption. When I read that my brain went "NOPE" and I realized I probably won't be sleeping tonight. Upvoted hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of my favorite SCP's. It's an amazing concept I've thought of for a while, something that gets relatively further away as you try to reach it. My only concern is that it claims the area of effect is "dome shaped".
Have they not tried tunneling? They cannot be certain it is dome shaped if they have not tried reaching it from underneath the "dome".
I'd recommend changing dome-shaped to spherical. Then add tunneling to the attempts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This could almost be turned into a novel about somebody (or multiple somebodies) trying to escape. Upvoted.

My only problem is "Any recordings collected [DATA EXPUNGED]." Why is information necessary for containment of the recordings exunged? It doesn't make sense to expunge something that people NEED to know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If I read something that sparks my interest and i want to know more about it, it usually gets an upvote.
This I want to know everything about, but sadly I only got one vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*reading comment*

"Wait, grass repeating in the picture? I just made that bit up for effect, it's just some random google image…"

*actually looks closely at picture*

"mother FUCK!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how if you look closely at the image of the radio tower, you can see the grass repeating. Utter terror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article was one of my favorites. Dealing with time and spacial distortion/glitchiness can be a delicate subject, so I'm pleased with how well it is written. Plus, a great example of how images can be used effectively.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For what it's worth, my immediate reaction to the sentence about the grass plane was, "Dammit, that's the wrong form of oh no it's not that's really quite clever."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, this was spawned from just such a conversation i had with Clef in the chat once…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the more excellent / memorable articles I've read. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do we still have the old image of the deer in the field kicking around here? It was a very effective image, and I'm wondering why it was removed from the page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ya know, rereading this, I realized this all sounds like the effect of lag in a video game. It's like real life lag. You jump around everywhere, end up walking into buildings, get stuck in a loop, people have trouble communicating…

Just sayin'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh hey, that was my idea. I think I discarded it after it being… I don't know. Maybe I should write it up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know what it reminded me of, a little? "It's a Good Life", so that I was wondering if there was going to be a cornfield somewhere. But yeah, it does have that strong Twilight Zone feel to it, doesn't it?

As an aside, I can't apparently italicize inside of the description text for "It's a Good Life", but I suppose I'll survive the lack. <laugh>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "FN-IO orders" is redundant in the same way "PIN number" is, so I changed it to something less redundant that sounded better than "for info on FN-IO, see this doc" to my ear. Also, eep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This would make an extremely excellent setting for some kind of Twilight Zone short. I would love to see the sky and surroundings snapping around like that in the background while some doomed heroes try to make sense of it all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember when we were all talking about this in the chat. Turned out pretty awesome and creepy. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, no…i posted the picture in chat, asking for help with ideas, and we started discussing "glitches" in reality, among other things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just out of curiosity… did this SCP evolve from the idea kicked around in chat about a bucket of "liquid" space-time that, when swirled, destroys what was inside it? Even if it isn't, this is a great one. And the pictures are total win.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good to hear…whenever I start screwing with major reality concepts, I start feeling nervous. Did you check out Log 084 A4 at the bottom as well?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually…it's a slight play-on-words. I think I'll leave it as-is for the moment, but if it turns out to be more annoying then clever, I'll make some alterations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fan-fucking-tastic. I love this article. I have a thing for spacial distortion based SCPs, and the second picture blew my mind. Gears, you've outdone yourself on this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the horror of being trapped in an essentially unchanging and unchangeable static existence. The way some of the things revert does kinda suggest some form of guiding intelligence (though not necessarily sapience), but that's just my feeling.

One question - were you going for "plane", as in "plane of existence", or "plain", as in "rolling, grassy plain"? I know it's a minor nitpick, but I tend to pick a lot of nits.

item.title: A question
item.content:encoded: Has anyone tried approaching it from above?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …send Snorlison in!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first new one in a good long while. I hope i haven't gotten too rusty, and thanks to the chat for the shot in the arm. This is a damn long work for me, but whenever I start screwing with time/space, I get long-winded. Any assistance or feedback would be VERY appreciated!
